# WebForge

> WebForge is a free, AI-ready Chrome extension by The Atom — plus a free online capture tool on the website — that turns any website into high-fidelity visual blueprints: full-page multi-viewport screenshots, a same-domain sitemap, and extracted CSS design tokens (colors, fonts, layouts) — structured for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT and Gemini.

Homepage: https://webforge.theatom.lk
Install / Source: https://github.com/zakisheriff/WebForge
Author: Zaki Sheriff (The Atom, Sri Lanka)
License / Cost: Free

## Core Capabilities

1. **Incremental Stitching Capture**: Scrolls dynamic pages, bypasses lazy-loading delays, hides scrollbars, and hides fixed/sticky headers to build a seamless full-page screenshot.
2. **Viewport Emulation**: Resizes the browser to capture authentic media queries across devices (Desktop 1440x900, Tablet 768x1024, Mobile 390x844).
3. **Domain Crawler**: Spawns sandboxed tabs to crawl and capture same-domain pages sequentially into a sitemap.
4. **Token Extractor**: Harvests the document's color palette (as hex) and font-family list.

## Online Capture Tool (no install)

The homepage hosts a free "Try WebForge on any URL" tool at https://webforge.theatom.lk/#try. Paste any website URL and it instantly renders the page across desktop (1440), tablet (768) and mobile (390) viewports, takes full-page screenshots, and extracts colors, fonts and image URLs — downloadable as a ZIP. The online tool captures a single page; the Chrome extension unlocks full-domain crawling, login/bot-protected pages, and pixel-perfect fidelity.

## Blueprint Output Schema

Every exported ZIP archive is a directory containing:
- `sitemap.json`: Array of crawled URLs.
- `metadata.json`: Global design tokens (combined fonts, colors, capture timestamp, page indexes).
- `pages/<slug>/`: One folder per captured page containing:
  - `desktop.png`, `tablet.png`, `mobile.png` (high-fidelity screenshots).
  - `metadata.json` (page title, URL, extracted fonts and colors).

## For LLM Context Loading

When rebuilding or studying layouts:
- Feed `metadata.json` to understand global styles.
- View the device PNG under `pages/<slug>/` as the visual blueprint.
- Reference extracted colors for exact HEX matching.

## Links

- Full details: https://webforge.theatom.lk/llms-full.txt
- Releases: https://github.com/zakisheriff/WebForge/releases
- Support: https://buymeacoffee.com/theoneatom
